entry.title: Need API policy for 404 condition
entry.content:
Hello Friends!!

I have a simple question that I haven't been able to answer myself 😞

I'm simply wanted to implement a policy that if the url is not a match I want to throw a 404.

However, the current state of my API policy allows any of the below to pass through to the end-point.

(correct) /cart/12068208/availability-check-url 

(invalid) /cart/12068208/availability

(invalid) /cart/12068208/availability-check-url-extra-characters

My condition is:

  <conditions>proxy.pathsuffix MatchesPath "/cart/{cart_id}/availability-check-url" OR proxy.pathsuffix MatchesPath "/cart/{cart_id}/availability-check-url/**" OR proxy.pathsuffix MatchesPath "/cart/{cart_id}/availability-check-url(**"</conditions>

I can generate a Fault if the condition matches - I can't figure out how to generate a fault if it doesn't.

Below is the full export of my API.

Any guidance would be appreciated.

 

 

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ProxyEndPoint default="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
    <name>default</name>
    <base_path>/v2/private</base_path>
    <properties/>
    <routeRules>
        <routeRule>
            <name>default</name>
            <targetEndPointName>default</targetEndPointName>
            <sequence>1</sequence>
            <faultRules/>
        </routeRule>
    </routeRules>
    <faultRules/>
    <preFlow>
        <name>PreFlow</name>
        <request>
            <isRequest>true</isRequest>
            <steps>
                <step>
                    <policy_name>GetServiceAuthenticationCredentials</policy_name>
                    <condition></condition>
                    <sequence>1</sequence>
                </step>
                <step>
                    <policy_name>InjectHeaderAuthenticationCredentials</policy_name>
                    <condition></condition>
                    <sequence>2</sequence>
                </step>
            </steps>
        </request>
    </preFlow>
    <postFlow>
        <name>PostFlow</name>
    </postFlow>
    <conditionalFlows>
        <conditionalFlow>
            <name>DefaultFaultFlow</name>
            <request>
                <isRequest>true</isRequest>
                <steps>
                    <step>
                        <policy_name>defaultRaiseFaultPolicy</policy_name>
                        <condition></condition>
                        <sequence>1</sequence>
                    </step>
                </steps>
            </request>
            <conditions>proxy.pathsuffix MatchesPath "/cart/{cart_id}/availability-check-url" OR proxy.pathsuffix MatchesPath "/cart/{cart_id}/availability-check-url/**" OR proxy.pathsuffix MatchesPath "/cart/{cart_id}/availability-check-url(**"</conditions>
            <readOnly>true</readOnly>
            <sequence>2</sequence>
        </conditionalFlow>
        <conditionalFlow>
            <name>cart/{cart_id}/availability-check-url</name>
            <request>
                <isRequest>true</isRequest>
				<steps>
                    <step>
                        <policy_name>resourceNotFoundPolicy</policy_name>
                        <condition></condition>
                        <sequence>1</sequence>
                    </step>
                </steps>
            </request>
            <conditions>(proxy.pathsuffix MatchesPath "/cart/{cart_id}/availability-check-url" OR proxy.pathsuffix MatchesPath "/cart/{cart_id}/availability-check-url/**" OR proxy.pathsuffix MatchesPath "/cart/{cart_id}/availability-check-url(**")AND(request.verb = "GET")</conditions>
            <readOnly>true</readOnly>
            <sequence>1</sequence>
        </conditionalFlow>
    </conditionalFlows>
</ProxyEndPoint>

entry.title: SAP ALM Roadshow Plano Dallas, Texas Wrap up March 6th 2025
entry.content:
We're excited to let you know of the profound success of the SAP ALM Roadshow 2025 that recently took place in Plano TX, NA. These events were a fantastic opportunity for enterprises to dive into the latest in Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) with SAP's experts, fellow industry leaders, and collaborative representatives from various SAP Lines of Business (Lobs).

 

Kick off and Keynote were given by Director, Premium Engagements – Delivery, South Mr. Prathish Jacob

Insights and industry trends on “ Clean Core and RISE with SAP Methodology ​ ​“ was delivered by Vice President, SAP Customer Services & Delivery Americas​ Mr. Karthik Thirumalai

A big thankyou to Mrs Kelly Stephen General Manager, Customer Services & Delivery, South at SAP

Below are some topics that we covered:

  • SAP Cloud ALM for GROW with SAP
  • SAP Cloud ALM for RISE with SAP and Clean Core Support
  • Implementing Intelligent Enterprise with SAP Cloud ALM ​
  • Automating testing with Tricentis Test Automation
  • Optimizing Operations combining AI and SAP Cloud ALM
  • Leveraging SAP Focused Run for Advanced monitoring capabilities ​
  • Transitioning from Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM​

Key Highlights:

  1. Innovative Workshops: Attendees participated in dynamic and interactive workshops that showcased the latest SAP ALM tools and methodologies tailored for the Noth American market.
  2. Expert Insights: Regional Lead RGTM NA LAC Mr Umesh Jagadesh gave various industry trends and experience how SAP Cloud ALM can be leveraged in the customers RISE and GROW journey. Also, Mrs Madison Mitrione (Business Process Consultant and POC for Preferred Success for SAP Cloud ALM) gave insights on various customer facing experience and industries trend in using and exploring SAP Cloud ALM.
  3. Regional Case Studies: Participants heard from local success stories where companies effectively implemented SAP ALM solutions to drive business growth and efficiency.
  4. Tech Demos: Live demonstrations of advanced SAP Cloud ALM features and related solutions that can propel your organization forward.
  5. Networking Sessions: Valuable networking opportunities allowed attendees to connect with peers, share experiences, and build professional relationships.
  6. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Representatives from other SAP LoBs, such as Cloud ERP, RISE with SAP, and BTP, contributed to the discussions, providing a holistic view of SAP’s ecosystem and how it integrates with SAP Cloud ALM.

 

Event Participation:

  • Plano Dallas, TX, 2025 March 6th, witnessed over 28 unique customers with discussions and collaborations enriching the experience. 

Looking Ahead: The feedback we’ve received Plano TX SAP ALM Roadshow Event has been overwhelmingly positive, reinforcing the importance of regional engagements to address specific market needs. We are excited about the ongoing conversation and collaboration within the Latin American tech ecosystem.

Conclusion: The SAP ALM Roadshow Plano TX  2025 has set a new standard for innovation and collaboration in NA region. We thank all the participants from both events, as well as our collaborative partners from Cloud ERP, RISE with SAP, and BTP, for helping make this a success. We look forward to building on this success together!! We also emphasized how customers and partners can leverage upcoming ALM summit at NSQ, NA March 26- 28

entry.title: Re: Issue with SAP ENABLE NOW Simulation Recording on SAP...
entry.content:
Hi Mickael,

If you are having issues recording Simulations in browser-based applications, such as Edge, there is a known current issue in Producer.  If your Rise application is running in Edge, look at this KBA for details about how you might address the issue until a fix is made available, likely in the May release this year.

After looking at the KBA, if you are still experiencing issues, please raise a support ticket using the Component ID KM-SEN-PRO and have the support team take a closer look.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: BPA SAP Desktop Agent v3.39.45. The job has been refused error.
entry.content:
Since the v3.39.45 update SAP Desktop Agent, I'm getting this error in Desktop agent.

The Job has been refused

This is happening for all my BPA projects, when launching a process or running the automation directly in design mode. Even a very simple one-step test project with a single dialog causes the error now.

So far tried the following:

  • Downgraded both the projects and Desktop Agent to the previous, working version. (3.38.59).
  • Removing and re-registering the agent in SAP Build
  • Uninstalling and re-installing Desktop Agent from scratch.

SAP Build Process Automation 

Any suggestions because at the moment we cannot run an BPA projects.

entry.title: Re: SAC Input control Issue
entry.content:
A possibility is if the CDS view isn't correctly exposing the key-value pair (e.g., @ObjectModel.text.association or @ObjectModel.text.element), SAC may not recognize members properly.

This can cause SAC to fail when trying to filter by member because it expects a properly associated key and text field.

entry.title: Fondo de ahorro MX, devolución del descuento en retro por faltas/incapacidades
entry.content:
Tengo un detalle, ya que cuando un empleado tiene una falta/incapacidad retroactiva,  La deducción es positiva por lo que no podemos, digamos así, solicitarle al banco que regrese aportación del empleado, ya que no debíamos descontarle su aportación (es mas tampoco deberíamos mandar la aportación Cia)

Si un empleado esta incapacitado la seman apasada y la corriente, por el retro de la semana pasada el sistema le regresa lo que le desconto de fondo de ahorro, como en la semana corriente tambien tiene la incapacidad no hay forma de que se netear el importe, 

Entonces si la semana pasada le descontro $100 pesos, ahora se la regresa, yaque el fondo de ahorro estaa en función de su sueldo/salario.

Espero puedan ayudarme, que es lo que estan haciendo ustedes, implementaron alguna regla ?, podrian describir su solución ?

 

De antemano gracias y saludos,

    Rogelio

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite API Management mTLS on API Proxy
entry.content:
Hi Venkat,

Thankfully, this is mostly Self-Service now.  SAP Integration Suite - Configuring Additional Virtual Host 

I am in contact with SAP at the moment to discuss some of these topics; however, the full chain of the public certificate must be loaded into the Configure > APIs > Certificates Tab in .pem or .der format and must be in leaf + intermediate(s) + root certificate order.

Once loaded there and your Virtual Host configured for mTLS, anyone who has the keypair of your public certificate should be able to access a proxy deployed on this VirtualHost.

One big caveat that I still have questions on:
I have received confirmation from SAP "certificate authentication" only checks to make sure the Intermediate/Root certificate matches a reference in the Certificates loaded into your trust store.  Meaning you may have loaded a certificate for Customer A signed by a CA (i.e. Digicert).  This means if Customer B uses the same CA as Customer A, they will also have access to an API Proxy on your VirtualHost even though you didn't load Customer B's certificate.

I tested this with SAP Integration Suite Certificates from two different environments (both signed by Digicert) and was able to make API Proxy calls for a certificate I didn't load to the trust store.

Therefore, the way around this is to create a RaiseFault policy that checks the content of the certificate; however, the only way to do this is by creating an SAP Ticket to enable the Client and Connection Properties on your VirtualHost.

All-in-all a bit cumbersome process; but mostly self-service.

entry.title: Re: how to build a live connection from SAC to S4HANA On...
entry.content:
The error message "We couldn't connect to your S/4HANA system..." when setting up a Live Connection from SAC to S/4HANA via Cloud Connector is typically caused by SSL certificate issues or network misconfigurations. Below are the troubleshooting steps to help resolve it.


---

1️⃣ Verify SSL Certificate Trust

Since SAC requires a trusted SSL certificate, if your S/4HANA system uses a self-signed or untrusted certificate, it may cause this issue.

🔹 Solution: Import the SSL Certificate

Open SAP Cloud Connector (SCC) UI (https://<SCC_HOST>:8443).

Go to Trust Manager and check if the SSL certificate of your S/4HANA system is trusted.

If not, manually import the SSL certificate from S/4HANA:

1. Open the S/4HANA URL in a browser (https://<S4_HOST>:<PORT>).


2. Export the SSL certificate (usually a .crt or .pem file).


3. In Cloud Connector UI, go to Administration > Certificate and Keys > Trusted Certificates.


4. Upload the exported SSL certificate and restart the SCC.

 

Also, ensure SAC trusts the SSL certificate:

1. Log into SAP Analytics Cloud.


2. Go to System > Administration > Security > Certificate Trust List.


3. Upload the same SSL certificate there.

 

 

---

2️⃣ Check Cloud Connector Status

Open SAP Cloud Connector (https://<SCC_HOST>:8443).

Go to Connector Status and ensure:

The connection to BTP is Green.

The connection to the backend system (S/4HANA) is Green.


If the status is Red, check:

The subaccount settings in SCC.

The mapping of internal/external hostnames.

The backend connection details.

 


---

3️⃣ Verify the Exposed Resources in Cloud Connector

In SAP Cloud Connector, go to:

Cloud to On-Premise > Access Control.

Ensure that S/4HANA is correctly exposed under Mapped System.

The Protocol should be HTTPS, and the Virtual Host/Port must match the live connection settings in SAC.


If using a Principal Propagation, ensure:

Identity Provider (IdP) is correctly configured.

The SAML assertion is properly mapped.

 


---

4️⃣ Validate the Connection in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)

Go to Connections in SAC and click Edit on the S/4HANA live connection.

Ensure that:

The Host/Port matches what is mapped in SCC.

The Authentication Type (e.g., "None", "Basic Authentication", or "Principal Propagation") is correct.

The Path Prefix is /sap/bw/ina/GetServerInfo (for INA-based connections).

 


---

5️⃣ Check Network & Proxy Settings

If your SAC instance is behind a firewall or proxy:

Whitelist the Cloud Connector’s IP in your firewall.

Check whether SAC can reach your S/4HANA system.

Run a traceroute from the Cloud Connector machine:

tracert <S4HANA_Host>

If network packets are blocked or failing, adjust firewall settings.

 


---

6️⃣ Enable Logging for More Debugging

On SAP Cloud Connector, go to:

Monitoring > Logs > Cloud Connector Logs.

Enable DEBUG mode and try reconnecting.

Look for connection errors in the logs.

7️⃣ Restart & Test Again

After making any changes:

1. Restart Cloud Connector (https://<SCC_HOST>:8443).

2. Restart the S/4HANA backend.

3. Retry creating the Live Connection in SAC.

entry.title: Mass Maintenance - Products taking a long time
entry.content:
Hi all,

We are trying to use Mass Maintenance - Products to update some material details (valuation areas in this case) and if the import file contains any more than around 10-20 records the process becomes extremely slow to the point that it stops entirely, even after running for days. There are no errors in the process when we only import a few changes at a time but it is not practical to do this when we need to make around 1700 changes.

Does anyone know what could be causing this problem, or what steps we can take to diagnose what is causing the performance issue?

Thanks

entry.title: Step-by-Step Guide: Installing SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 (aka SAP BI 2025)
entry.content:
Get ready for SAP BI 2025!

Welcome to this comprehensive tutorial on installing SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025, the latest evolution of SAP's trusted business intelligence platform.

As someone who has been working with this technology for over 25 years and writing installation guides since SAP BI 4.1 back in 2013, I know how important a smooth setup process is. This guide will walk you through every step, complete with screenshots and useful links, so you can get SAP BI 2025 up and running without any headaches.

I still can't believe SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 is here!  Bringing a fresh wave of enhancements that make business intelligence more efficient, secure, and future-ready.  Let’s do this!

Before you start

Here are some useful links I think are worth reading first before going ahead and getting technical!

🚀What's New:

  • Welcome to SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 by @GregBotticchio
  • Experience Your Favourite BI Platform, BOBJ, in a Fresh New Look with the SAP BI 2025 Release by @VenkateswaraGupthaY
  • SAP BI 2025: What’s New In Web Intelligence and Semantic Layer by @christian_ah-soon 
  • SAP BI 2025: What’s New in Crystal Reports by @donald_guo 
  • Official What's New (SAP Help)

📝Strategy:

  • SAP Analytics Business Intelligence Statement of Direction
  • SAP BI On Premise Maintenance Strategy
  • BI 2025 Maintenance Strategy & Schedule (SAP Note)

General Availability: 12.03.2025 (Version: 14.4.0.5253)
End of Mainstream Maintenance: 31.12.2027

 ⚙️ Technical:

  • 🔥 Supported Platforms (PAM) 
  • ⚠️ Products and features are removed in SAP BusinessObjects BI platform 2025 (SAP Notes)
  • 🆘 SAP Business Intelligence Suite Release Restrictions
  • 💾 SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 Release Notes
  • User Guides (SAP Help)
  • Installation & Upgrade Guides (SAP Help)
  • Administrator's Guides (SAP Help)

Technical Assessment

Run a Free SAP BI 2025 Readiness Assessment

Wiiisdom offers a free Customer Cockpit in 360Suite that helps assess your organization's readiness for SAP BI 2025. This tool provides valuable statistical insights about your environment, including:

  • The number of UNV universes that will need to be converted to UNX.
  • The number of Crystal Reports for Enterprise that must be converted to Crystal Reports "Classic" or Web Intelligence.
  • The number of Lumira visualizations that will need to be rebuilt in SAP Analytics Cloud or Web Intelligence.
  • The number of Live Office documents that must be rebuilt in SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office or Web Intelligence.
  • The number of documents in Analysis for OLAP that will need to be rebuilt in SAP Analytics Cloud or Web Intelligence.

In addition, the solution provides insights into your Universes' usage, such as:

  • The number of documents still pointing to UNV universes (these will need to be repointed).
  • The number of UNX Multi Source Universes (MSU) that will need to be converted to Single Source Universes (SSU).

Pre-Requisites

License Keys

Before proceeding with a new installation, upgrade, or migration of SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025, it is essential to generate a new License Key. Even if you are upgrading from a previous version, a fresh license key is required to activate BI 2025 successfully.

In addition to reviewing the provided documents and links, make sure to complete this step before installation to avoid any activation issues.

This actually might be the most complicated part of this entire project!

For guidance on generating your SAP BI 2025 license key, refer to the following links:

  • 📌 How to generate license keys for SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence Platform (SAP Notes)
  • 📌 SAP provides Emergency License Keys valid for 90-days in case there are issues in obtaining your permanent keys.  Make sure to read the important notes at the top of the PDF.

Software Download

Finding all the necessary installation files for SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 can sometimes be a bit tricky. If you're having trouble locating them, don't worry—SAP provides a helpful SAP Note that outlines exactly where to download everything you need.

📌 How to download SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence (BI) software (SAP Notes)

📌 Shortcut to SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 Download page

Installation Steps

The steps outlined below are specific to the Windows installation, but the process for Linux is very similar; please refer to the official documentation for more detailed instructions.

The first step is to launch the installer. You can execute the initial file you downloaded, "BIPLATS4400_0-80008823_P1.EXE," or, personally, I prefer using a tool like WinRAR to extract the file to a folder first and then run "setup.exe" to start the installation. In both cases, the second file, "BIPLATS4400_0-80008823_P2.RAR," will be used automatically. Choose whichever method you prefer!

 

 

 

As you can see here, the prerequisite check failed because I needed to address a suppressed reboot. Whatever the issues may be, they will need to be resolved before you can continue.

 

 

 

Although you can change your mind later and select additional languages, I strongly recommend making your decision during the initial installation to avoid lengthy steps and unnecessary risks.

Based on my experience, unless I only require a Web Server, selecting the Web Tier is perfect for the job. In all other cases, I opt for a Full installation and, if necessary, manually disable the Tomcat service. While this may waste some disk space and take a little more time during updates, it ensures that you have a Web Tier available on that application server for troubleshooting purposes.

I would never choose a Custom / Expand installation. In the end, you'll only save a bit of disk space, but you'll reduce your flexibility later on.

Remember: SAP doesn't recommend using the default SQL Anywhere Database for BI in Production.  SAP Note.  Here I'll be using Microsoft SQL Server Express 2022 but below is the screen you'd get if you decided to stick with Sybase SQL Anywhere.

It requires some technical skills but it is possible to change your mind later on and use a different database for the System Database without losing your data.  You'd be doing this in the Central Configuration Manager (CCM).  It's a different story for the Audit Database...

 

 

 

 

Note: In this case, I created my ODBC connections first.  If you forget, you can go ahead at this stage and click Refresh to load them in the installer.

 

 

Note: The SIA Port and the same below for the CMS and Tomcat can be changed easily after the installation.

 

Note: Make sure you keep all these passwords in a vault or something.  The CMS Cluster Key is needed for a number of things like clustering servers for instance.

 

 

 

 

 

 🥳 And... Done

About screen from the Central Management Console (CMC):

 About screen from the BI launch pad:

Post-Installation Configuration

When logging into the Central Management Console (CMC) for the first time, you will be prompted with the System Configuration Wizard. While this can be done at a later stage and is available from the main menu, I recommend completing it sooner rather than later.

In short, this wizard will guide you through the process of splitting and configuring your Adaptive Processing Servers (APS). The more RAM you have, the more options you'll be able to configure.

This isn't a new wizard, but I’m always surprised to see production environments where the APS aren’t optimally configured. This is a great place to start, and be sure to consult the administrator's and sizing guides for more detailed information.

 

 

 

Note: As you can see, selecting 15-25 GB RAM will create 7 APS on this node.  The default installation only has 1 APS.

 

 

 

 

 

Conclusion

I'm sure you're as excited as I am about this new release, and I hope this guide has been helpful to you.

There’s a lot of great documentation out there, but it can be all over the place and difficult to find. My goal with these guides remains the same: to provide what I think are the most useful and important resources all in one place.

Feel free to share your comments and suggestions!

Thanks, and take care.

entry.title: Page Footer Supress Does Not Work Properly
entry.content:
This is a problem I've had since 2011 at the beginning of Crystal Reports, and it still occurs today in the 2020 SP4/P4 version (14 year of problem).

When a page footer is defined and suppressed without formula, the detail takes advantage of the entire sheet with the records.

When it is suppressed via formula, it does not take over the space.

SAP Crystal Reports

entry.title: 资产会计 - SAP S4HANA Cloud（公共版）2502 版本中的资产重估新功能介绍
entry.content:
背景介绍

我们会计记录的都是历史成本，但是随着社会经济的发展，会计上记录的过去的金额不能反映真实公允的价值了，因此准则允许企业进行重估。例如2010年工厂购买的商业地产，记录在账面上的价值是30万元，但是2020的时候价值就变成了60万，那么现在账面上记录的30万元就不能真实公允的体现出实际的价值，所以需要重估。

对于手动资产重估，可使用“过账资产重估”应用程序 (F7775)。之前，您可以同时以所有或多个货币重估资产。通常，以多种货币执行重估。

但是，在某些情况下，您只需要以本币执行重估，而不会影响集团货币（硬通货币）。

新业务交易类型“以所选货币重估”可用于此目的。使用此新业务交易类型，可以单一（所选）货币过账重估并报告。

这意味着您现在可以区分标准重估情况（所有受影响的货币）和特定重估（所有分类账中只有一种货币）。

测试场景

资产于 2024 年 12 月 31 日购置，折旧码为 CD01。使用年限为 4 年。折旧方法为直线折旧。残值率百分比为 5%

购置和生产成本交易(APC):

100,000

2025年计划普通折旧:

-23,750

2025年底净账面值:

76,250

资产购置之后

2025年的计划折旧金额的计算公式是100000*(1-5%)/48*12=23750

由于通货膨胀，需要在 2024 年末进行重估：资产的账面价值将重估 20%。

在过账资产重估应用的选择屏幕上，指定以下值：

例如：

  • 20,000 APC 的重估
  • 5000 的普通折旧的重估

对于手动重估，可以使用过账资产重估应用 (F7775)。之前，您可以使用该应用同时以所有或多个货币重估。通常以多种货币执行重估。

但是，在某些情况下，您需要仅以本币执行重估，而集团货币（硬通货币）不受影响。

2502版本支持对于特定重估（所有分类账中仅一种货币），在字段业务交易类型选择“重估(所选货币)”

资产购置和重估之后

经过重估之后，2025年的计划折旧金额的计算公式是[(100000+20000）*(1-5%)-5000)/48*12=27250

除手动重估外，自 2502 版本起，新应用维护资产重估指数引入了有关在特定折旧范围中使用重估指数自动重估的功能。

如果要定期执行基于指数值的自动重估，则使用此应用。

可在链接中找到详细说明

  1. 创建重估索引（强制）

2.将索引标识分配到资产主记录（必填）

3.检查固定资产评估视图中的价值（可选）

4.检查资产历史数据表中的值（可选）

此处共享步骤的一些结果:

步骤 1：创建重估索引（必填）

步骤 2：将索引标识分配到资产主记录（必填）

步骤 3：检查管理固定资产应用的评估视图中的值（可选）

根据分配给资产的重估索引，重估金额和折旧金额将按如下设计进行计划。

对于2024年

对于2025年

对于2026年

对于2027年

步骤 4：检查资产历史数据表中的值（可选）

总结

这一新功能使得管理资产重估更加灵活，使您能够更有效地处理特定重估场景。

entry.title: Integration Insights Live: Innovate with a modern iPaaS
entry.content:
Leading businesses find the core of generating tangible outcomes from investments in enterprise AI is an integrated business landscape using integration as a service (iPaaS). Integrations make scaling AI across enterprises possible because it allows democratic information sharing across users regardless of the systems and apps used in the enterprise and no matter where the teams are located.  

Today, it is well understood that only by connecting the multitude of solutions and facilitating seamless information exchange can you truly unlock intelligence, simplify innovation, and lower your total cost of ownership. The approach of building point-to-point integrations to connect individual software systems is long gone. It lacked scale and introduced a whole new layer of complexity where every new system being added to the landscape required custom code or connectors to communicate with existing systems. Integration as a service allows you to extract maximum value from the enterprise platforms that have been invested and drive real results.

With SAP Integration Suite on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) you can easily address your integration needs using modern enterprise integration patterns such as API-based and Event-driven integrations. To learn more about its extensive functionality, join us at our upcoming virtual summit - Integration insights live: Innovate with a modern iPaaS. We’ve lined up five sessions exploring the key features, sharing real-world use cases, discussing value benefits, as well as providing an overview of the latest innovations and exciting road map.

Keynote

AI, modernization, and automation initiatives are top of mind for many IT leaders today as they grapple with increasing application landscape diversity and resource constraints. So what technologies are used to drive success of these projects? Hear Shari Lava, senior director, AI and automation  at IDC and Achim Kraiss, chief product officer for SAP Business Technology Platform discuss the strategic value of an iPaaS.

CONA Services: Accelerating innovation with SAP Integration Suite

This inspiring session features our customer CONA Services LLC, an IT services company for the 12 largest independently owned Coca-Cola bottling companies in North America. You’ll hear firsthand how the organization boosted bottlers’ business growth and competitive advantage by integrating multiple solutions and third-party applications on SAP Integration Suite.

Unleash the power of AI-driven integration for complex application landscapes

For a holistic view of integration scenarios with scalable, real-time integration of business processes, APIs, and events across SAP and third-party landscapes. I will be the speaker of this session. Do join in to learn about the comprehensive capabilities offered by SAP Integration Suite.

Connect all your applications with prebuilt integrations and adapters

If you’re struggling with a growing landscape of diverse applications and it’s making connected business processes a challenge, this session is for you! SAP Integration Suite offers hundreds of prebuilt adapters and connectors to many other applications such as those from Salesforce, ServiceNow, NetSuite, Snowflake, and more. Allowing you to connect your applications quickly, with these adapters you can avoid using multiple integration solutions for different applications.

Solving connectivity chaos to improve AI outcomes

These days, modern integration tools don’t just solve today’s complex connectivity challenges, they also enable improved AI outcomes. IDC’s Shari Lava returns to the stage to share her research findings on how modern integration platforms unlock business value from AI investments.

Don’t miss this opportunity to learn more about SAP Integration Suite and how it can support your integration needs. Whether you are looking to elevate operational efficiency or deliver digital transformation and improve AI outcomes, simplified integration is your key to success.

Register now to secure your spot. We look forward to seeing you there!

entry.title: Re: How to defining credit master data in the business pa...
entry.content:
It’s a guidance on Defining Credit Master Data in the Business Partner.

  1. Go to “Manage Your Solution”, select the Application Area as “Finance” and the Sub Application Area as “Credit Evaluation and Management”, in the Master Data, please make the following configuration:
    a. Create Credit Segments
    b. Create Risk Classes
    c. Create Rule for Scoring and Credit Limit Calculation


    In Process and Business Transactions, make the following configuration:
    a. Define Checking Rules


  2. Navigate to app Schedule Credit Management Jobs, and create with the job template Assign Credit Management Role to Business Partners.

entry.title: Re: #CoE#LAL Cannot create sales order with customer down...
entry.content:
Hi Patryk, if free goods item category is TANN( it's the line item that generated automatically when the count of goods reach the restriction of discount type YA00), it does have price but 100% discount. Whether it can see as a kind of sales?

Such as CBXN, this kind of item has price and 100% discount, so this item has billing. And CBLN, it's also free goods, but it doesn't has price , so no billing for that.

If TANN can be seen as a kind of sales, so it should has billing?

entry.title: Heatmap-Driven Testing for SAP S/4HANA: A Systematic Approach Using UiPath Test Suite
entry.content:
Software testing in SAP S/4HANA projects often requires extensive collaboration between functional teams, business analysts, and quality assurance professionals to validate business-critical processes comprehensively. However, given the large number of transactions in an SAP system, manually prioritizing test cases can lead to inefficiencies, delays, and gaps in coverage.

This article explores how UiPath Test Suite’s SAP Heatmap can be leveraged as a systematic method for test case generation by identifying high-risk transactions based on key system attributes.

A heatmap-based approach to test design is rooted in risk-based testing (RBT) principles, where test identification and prioritization are determined by quantifiable risk indicators. The UiPath SAP Heatmap, an integrated feature of Test Manager, provides a visual representation of transactional importance based on the following factors:

  • User Engagement (#Users) – Number of users interacting with a transaction (T-Code, Fiori app, or report).
  • Execution Frequency (#Executions) – How often a transaction is executed within a defined period.
  • Processing Time (Performance) – The cumulative system processing time for each transaction.
  • Complexity (#Objects) – The number of SAP development objects involved (e.g., screens, functional modules, tables).
  • Transaction Type (Intensity) – Classification of transactions as Create, Change, or Display operations.

The heatmap thereby serves as an empirical model for test case selection, ensuring that testing efforts are focused on transactions with the highest business and technical significance.

By leveraging heatmap insights, QA teams can focus their testing where it matters most - transactions with the highest business impact and technical complexity.

After generating the SAP Heatmap, teams can filter by module (e.g., Finance, Logistics, Procurement) to identify areas that require testing. For this example, we focus on the Logistics module.

The heatmap visually scales transaction importance—the larger the transaction in the heatmap, the more frequently it is used. Let’s choose MMBE (Stock Overview) - a heavily utilized transaction in inventory management.

With a single click on "Generate Tests", UiPath Test Suite automatically creates structured test cases. To enhance test accuracy and traceability, testers can integrate supporting documentation and prompts into the test case repository, including:

✔ BPMN Diagrams – Business process flow models.
✔ SAP Functional Documentation – Blueprints outlining process expectations.
✔ Test Data Definitions – Pre-configured input datasets.
✔ Prompts – Define testing goals such as system/ integration/ regression

These artifacts provide a comprehensive reference for both manual and automated test execution.

You will now have a list of detailed, structured test cases generated, including:

✔ Test Steps – A step-by-step breakdown of the transaction flow.
✔ Expected Results – Pass/fail conditions per step.
✔ Test Data – Reusable input values.
✔ Automation Potential – Readiness for automated execution.

 

This article focused on a data-driven approach enabled by UiPath Test Suite, to streamline test case generation based on system usage patterns, transaction frequency, and business criticality. By systematically identifying high-risk transactions, QA teams can:

✔ Optimize test resource allocation.
✔ Reduce overall testing effort and costs.
✔ Enhance test automation readiness.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Text in manually added columns
entry.content:
appreciating your response @nikhil Here is a screenshot of the calculated column "Business Plan 2025."

The column is calculated by summing the Outlook and Business Plan 2025 values from the BW Query, which is currently hidden. This column has been derived by applying a simple summation formula. The heading for this column is part of a dimension labeled as "Category."

However, the category columns are the original ones that have been replaced with manually created columns. If the dimension is used to generate dynamic text, the original column reappears. Therefore, the goal is to make it dynamic based on the fiscal year dimension.

entry.title: Re: Functional Currency differs from Company Code Currency
entry.content:
Hi Derek,

Before release, viewing the standard cost calculation in the functional currency is not possible.
For the material cost estimate, we support Controlling Area Currency and Object/Company Code Currency, which applies to all deployment options.
Upon release, the cost is translated into all parallel currencies.

Material cost estimates are calculated and stored in the Controlling Area Currency and Object Currency, then translated into the additional currencies defined for the ledger at the time of release.

Regards,

Venu

entry.title: Beyond SAP Cloud - Episode 10: BTP Cloud Foundry Spaces
entry.content:
In this episode, we explore the concept of spaces within SAP's Business Technology Platform (BTP). Spaces allow for organized development environments within sub-accounts, enabling selective access to applications, such as HR or finance, ensuring sensitive data is secured. We differentiate spaces from subaccounts, focusing on the benefits of spaces for project management and access control. Additionally, we highlight the importance of memory quota management for spaces, with a look ahead to further exploration of quota configurations in the next episode. Spaces offer a streamlined way to manage resources and permissions efficiently.

 

Takeaways

  • Spaces in BTP allow additional organizational levels for Cloud Foundry runtime in subaccounts.
  • Cloud Foundry runtime offers the ability to create spaces for specific project deployments.
  • Developers can be given restricted access to specific spaces, ensuring security for sensitive applications.
  •  The main difference between a space and a subaccount is that business users are associated with subaccounts, not spaces.
  • In scenarios where identifying different business users is needed, a new subaccount is required.
  • If business user identification is not necessary, adding an additional space is recommended.
  • The same Cloud connector can be used in multiple spaces, benefiting from the same configuration in the sub-account.
  • Roles and trust configurations are managed at the subaccount level, not the space level.
  • Spaces require configuration of quotas, including memory allocation for Cloud Foundry.
  • Quotas in spaces provide full control over the memory consumed by Cloud Foundry in each space.
  • The next episode will dive deeper into how to assign quotas for better resource management.

_SAP_TEC_USR... This is the user who accesses the HANA DB behind the scenes and I am aware of it. Is it safe to lock it?
BGRFC_SUPER... This is the recognition used for the RFC connection. Is it safe to lock it?
